answer:
**The Falsafa corpus engages the concept of revelation primarily through the Hindu notion of *śruti* (revealed scripture, i.e., the Vedas as divinely heard), which is treated as the highest authority on *dharma* and ritual validity, superior to *smṛti* (tradition).**[^1][^2][^3] No works directly discuss *wahy* (Qur'anic revelation) or "divine speech" as such; searches for those terms and related phrases like "divine revelation" or "divine inspiration" yield zero results. Mentions of "prophet" appear poetically in Allama Iqbal's *Bang-e-Dara* (Islamic context) and Cynewulf's *Andreas* (Christian prophets), but without substantive treatment of revelation mechanics.[^4]

**Key works and their engagement:**

- **Manusmṛti**: Defines *śruti* as the Veda (revealed) and *smṛti* as its subordinate; calls certain rules "Vedic revelation" (e.g., ritual timing); urges adherence for spiritual reward.[^5][^6][^7]

- **Yājñavalkya Smṛti**: Positions revelation (*śruti*, the Vedas) as a root of *dharma* alongside tradition and virtuous conduct; prescribes conduct "prescribed by revelation (*śruti*) and tradition (*smṛti*)"; elevates Vedic study.[^8][^9][^10]

- **Āṅgirasa Smṛti**: Defines *dharma* via scriptural injunctions (*codanā*) known through *śruti* indicators (e.g., verbal moods), which grant liberation; repeatedly invokes *śruti* for rites, expiation, ancestor satisfaction, and validity.[^11][^12]

**Other minor engagements:**
- **Bṛhaspati Smṛti**: References *smṛti* as tradition but ties legal norms to Vedic/*smṛti* rites indirectly.[^13]
- No Abrahamic or other non-Hindu systematic treatments of revelation found.

[^1]: Multiple Dharmaśāstra works, e.g., [Āṅgirasa Smṛti, Ch. 1](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-07cb57).

[^2]: [Manusmṛti, Ch. 2](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-a3859b).

[^3]: [Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 1](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-7d795b).

[^4]: E.g., [Bang-E-Dara Part 3](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/04-dil-se-jo-baat-nikalti-hai-asar-rakhti-hai/translation/#p-d22740); [Andreas, Ch. 1](https://falsafa.corpus/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-bb7698).

[^5]: [Manusmṛti, Ch. 2, ¶p-a3859b](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-a3859b).

[^6]: [Manusmṛti, Ch. 1, ¶p-47b537](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-47b537).

[^7]: [Manusmṛti, Ch. 2](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/).

[^8]: [Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 1, ¶p-7d795b](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-7d795b).

[^9]: [Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 1, ¶p-25ce19](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-25ce19).

[^10]: [Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 1](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/).

[^11]: [Āṅgirasa Smṛti, Ch. 1, ¶p-07cb57](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-07cb57).

[^12]: [Āṅgirasa Smṛti, Ch. 1](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/).

[^13]: [Bṛhaspati Smṛti, Ch. 1](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/).
